Unified Threat Console

Integrated Cyber Security Monitoring & Threat Detection

Unified Threat Console

Integrated Cyber Security Monitoring and Threat Detection System
A Mini SOC Platform for Centralized Security Operations

Python • FastAPI • Web Dashboard
Introduction

Cybersecurity Monitoring
in Modern Networks

As organizations grow their digital infrastructure, the attack surface expands rapidly. Every connected device, application, and service introduces potential vulnerabilities that adversaries can exploit to breach systems and steal sensitive data.

Effective cybersecurity requires continuous monitoring of network traffic, proactive vulnerability identification, and rapid incident response. Organizations deploy multiple tools to achieve these goals, but lack of integration creates critical blind spots.

The challenge lies not in the absence of security tools, but in their fragmented deployment and disconnected operation across the security infrastructure.
Problem Statement

Fragmented Security Creates Gaps

🔌

Separate Monitoring Tools

Organizations deploy independent tools for network monitoring, intrusion detection, vulnerability scanning, and log analysis — each with its own interface, data format, and alert mechanism.

⏰

Delayed Threat Detection

When security tools operate in isolation, correlating threat intelligence across systems becomes manual and slow. Critical threats go unnoticed for hours or even days.

🔒

Poor Coordination

Security teams must switch between multiple dashboards and consoles to investigate incidents. This context-switching reduces efficiency and increases response time.

⚠

Inefficient Response

Without a unified view of security events, incident response is reactive rather than proactive. Teams struggle to prioritize threats and allocate resources effectively.

The Need

Why a Centralized
Security Operations Center?

  • Security Operations Centers (SOCs) provide a unified command post for monitoring, detecting, and responding to security threats across the entire organization.
  • Enterprise SOCs are expensive and complex to deploy, making them inaccessible for small organizations and academic institutions that need security visibility.
  • A lightweight, self-hosted Mini SOC platform bridges this gap — delivering core SOC capabilities without the infrastructure overhead of enterprise solutions.
  • Centralized correlation of security events dramatically improves detection accuracy and reduces mean time to respond (MTTR) for security incidents.
Project Overview

Unified Threat Console (UTC)

A Python-based backend system with a locally hosted web dashboard that integrates multiple security modules into a single, cohesive platform.

🔌

Network Monitor

Live packet capture & analysis

🛡

Intrusion Detection

Rule-based threat detection

🔎

Vuln Scanner

Web vulnerability assessment

📋

Log Monitor

Event collection & alerting

🔒

File Transfer

AES-256 secure transfer

🎯

Attack Simulator

Controlled attack testing

💻

Dashboard

SOC-style web interface

🚀

Real-time

WebSocket live updates

UTC consolidates seven core security modules into one platform, replacing multiple disconnected tools with a unified backend that shares threat intelligence and generates correlated alerts.

Architecture

System Architecture
& Technology Stack

Backend Python 3 + FastAPI REST API
Frontend HTML / CSS / JavaScript Web UI
Database SQLite Structured Storage
Real-time WebSockets Live Updates
Security AES-256 + Token Auth Encryption
Module 1

Network Traffic
Monitoring

Packet Capture

Live network packet capture using raw sockets, extracting IP addresses, ports, and protocol information from every packet in real time.

Protocol Analysis

Deep packet inspection identifies TCP, UDP, DNS, and ICMP protocols with payload metadata for comprehensive traffic visibility.

Anomaly Detection

Statistical analysis flags suspicious traffic patterns and unusual volume spikes that may indicate reconnaissance or attack activity.

Live Dashboard

Real-time packet data visualization on the web dashboard with filtering, search, and protocol-based categorization capabilities.

Module 2

Intrusion Detection System

Rule-based detection engine with stateful tracking and time-window analysis for real-time threat identification.

High

Port Scanning Detection

Identifies sequential port scans by tracking connection attempts across multiple ports from a single source within a defined time window.

High

Brute Force Attempts

Detects repeated authentication failures from the same IP address, flagging potential credential stuffing or password cracking attacks.

Critical

DoS / DDoS Patterns

Monitors traffic volume per source and identifies denial-of-service patterns through threshold-based analysis and packet rate tracking.

Medium

Suspicious Port Activity

Flags connections to known malicious ports (4444, 1337, etc.) commonly associated with backdoors, reverse shells, and C2 communication.

Module 3

Web Vulnerability
Scanner

1SQL Injection — Payload injection with response analysis
2Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) — Reflected and stored XSS checks
3Security Headers — Missing headers like CSP, HSTS, X-Frame
4Open Redirect — Parameter-based redirect testing
5Directory Traversal — Path traversal vulnerability probes
Informational

Low-risk findings

Potential Risk

Needs review

Confirmed

Vulnerability found

Module 4

Log Monitoring & Alert System

Centralized log collection, structured storage, and anomaly detection across all security modules.

14:23:05 CRIT Port scan detected from 192.168.1.105 — 47 ports in 12s
14:22:48 WARN Brute force attempt on SSH — 8 failed logins from 10.0.0.22
14:21:30 WARN SQL Injection detected on target.com/search?q=test'
14:20:15 INFO Vulnerability scan completed — 3 informational, 2 potential risks
14:19:00 INFO Network interface eth0 captured 1,247 packets in 60s window
14:18:22 CRIT Suspicious port 4444 connection from external IP blocked
Modules 5 & 6

Secure File Transfer & Attack Simulator

🔒 Secure File Transfer Module 5

▶AES-256 encryption ensures file confidentiality during storage and transfer, making intercepted data unreadable without the correct decryption key.
▶Token-based access control with optional password protection adds multiple layers of authentication before granting file access.
▶Configurable expiry times automatically invalidate file access after a set duration, reducing the window of exposure for sensitive documents.
▶Integrity verification ensures that files have not been tampered with during storage or transmission, maintaining data trustworthiness.

🎯 Attack Simulator Module 6

▶Simulates port scanning attacks to demonstrate IDS detection capabilities, generating realistic traffic patterns that trigger security alerts.
▶Traffic flooding simulation tests the system's ability to detect and respond to denial-of-service attack patterns in real time.
▶Operates within a strictly controlled environment to prevent any impact on production systems or external networks.
▶Provides educational value by demonstrating how each attack type is detected and classified by the IDS engine.
System Workflow

End-to-End Data Flow Pipeline

Step 1

Network Capture

Raw packet ingestion

→
Step 2

IDS Analysis

Rule-based detection

→
Step 3

Log & Alert

Correlated events

→
Step 4

Dashboard

Visual display

Module 7

Centralized Web
Dashboard

A professional SOC-style web interface that provides a single pane of glass view for all security operations. Built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, the dashboard offers both light and dark themes with real-time updates via WebSocket connections.

✓Live traffic monitoring with packet-level detail and protocol filtering
✓Real-time threat alerts with severity classification and source tracking
✓Comprehensive log viewer with search, filter, and anomaly highlighting
✓Vulnerability scan results with risk classification and remediation hints
✓WebSocket-driven real-time updates without manual page refresh
Assessment

Advantages, Limitations & Future Scope

✓

Advantages

  • Centralized monitoring from a single unified console
  • Real-time threat detection and instant alerting
  • Improved visibility via cross-module data correlation
  • User-friendly web interface requiring minimal training
  • Modular and scalable architecture design
⚠

Limitations

  • Limited to basic rule-based detection only
  • Requires admin privileges for packet capture
  • Not enterprise-grade — mini SOC only
  • Single-user architecture limits collaboration
  • SQLite may face performance issues at scale
🚀

Future Scope

  • ML-based anomaly and behavioral detection
  • Cloud deployment for distributed monitoring
  • Integration with MITRE ATT&CK and CVE feeds
  • Multi-user auth and role-based access control
  • Automated incident response playbooks
